content.question: Are the PISA performance gains measured in Poland after its 1999 reform attributable primarily to delayed selection and extended general education, or do they reflect selection-out of lowest-performing students from assessment, demographic changes, or concurrent unrelated policy reforms?
content.reduction_path: Obtain detailed Polish PISA microdata and student labor market outcome data across the 1999 reform cohort boundary; conduct subgroup analysis tracking performance of students who would have been in vocational tracks pre-1999 but remained in general education post-1999; compare Poland's 1999 PISA gains to contemporaneous reforms in other countries with similar starting-point disparities; review Polish curriculum and assessment methodology changes in 1999-2000 period to rule out assessment-driven gains.
